tool.name: distmat
tool.summary: Pairwise p-distance matrix for equal-length sequence sets

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: `distmat` computes a deterministic p-distance matrix for one local sequence set. The current EMBOSS-RS implementation uses the shared sequence loader and reports mismatch fraction as `mismatches / sequence_length` for each pair in stable input order.

sections.title: Inputs
sections.content: The current v1 interface accepts exactly one local sequence-set input path. All records must have equal length before the distance matrix can be derived.

sections.title: Outputs
sections.content: CLI and service output is a stable square table with one row per input record and one column per input record. Diagonal entries are zero, and off-diagonal entries report pairwise mismatch fraction as fixed-precision decimal text.

sections.title: Current Status
sections.content: This method is implemented and exposed through `emboss-rs distmat`. Current Rust service coverage exercises the committed equal-length three-record FASTA fixture and locks down the expected p-distance rows in stable source order.

sections.title: Caveats
sections.content: Mixed-length inputs fail validation instead of being padded, aligned, or compared over partial overlap. The first release reports simple p-distance only; it does not yet expose alternate distance models.

artifacts.label: Equal-length FASTA fixture
artifacts.description: Repository-managed three-record FASTA fixture with equal-length sequences used for deterministic p-distance validation.

examples.title: Compute a p-distance matrix for three equal-length FASTA records
examples.description: Loads the committed three-record FASTA fixture and reports the pairwise mismatch fractions in stable input order.

expected_outputs.label: P-distance matrix table
expected_outputs.description: A square table with zero diagonal entries and fixed-precision mismatch fractions for each record pair.
